equations_checked.notes: Reduced-modulus definition was visually checked at 300 dpi; omitted because elastic-property extraction is not a core mechanism for the present routing task.
Indentation-hardness definition was visually checked and omitted as standard background.
Reduced-to-Young's-modulus relation and diamond properties were checked; not retained because the evidence card focuses on fracture and damage.
Retained as E1. COF = FT/FV and force directions were checked against Fig. 5.
Retained as E2. Numerator FT and denominator sqrt(2 p(d) A(d)) were visually checked. The homogeneous-material and horizontal-crack assumptions are preserved.
Retained in E3 as fs(d) = 2 p(d) A(d).
Ideal spherical-probe shape function was visually checked; omitted because the authors show that the actual R10 requires the general calibrated Eq. (8).
Retained in E3. R^3 multiplier, cubic/quadratic/linear terms and nonnegative coefficient constraints were checked. Fitted alpha=298.1, delta=0, gamma=0 and R2=0.99 were checked in text and Fig. 6.
Retained in E4. Negative sign, squared principal-stress difference and k(sigma1+sigma3) denominator were visually checked at 300 dpi.
Retained in E4 as maximum of tensile Rankine strain and compression-dominant equivalent strain.
Retained in E5 as the linear traction-opening softening law.
Retained in E5 as isotropic-damage stress relation sigma=(1-omega)E epsilon_tilde.
Retained in E5. Both multiplicative factors and exponent -1 were visually checked; h is the crack-band element length and Gf is Mode-I fracture energy.

Retained as E6 exactly in printed order: Gf = Kc^2/E/(1-nu^2) for plane strain. The expression is visually legible but its operation order conflicts with the usual plane-strain effective-modulus form; it is marked unusable pending source-data or author confirmation.
Contact-pressure correction for fitted compacted tensile strength was checked; omitted because it is an empirical normalization tied to the first load level, not a transferable material law.
Normalized compacted tensile-strength definition was checked and omitted; its non-portability is retained as a warning.

tables_checked.notes: C-4m groove widths and phase-resolved Kc values at 25, 50 and 100 mN were checked.
C-6y groove widths, phase-resolved Kc values at 5-100 mN and homogenized Kc at 200-500 mN were checked; the final 0.64 +/- 0.14 MPa sqrt(m) value was verified.
C-4m FE inputs and outputs were checked, including E, Gf, d, FT, fitted compacted tensile strength, crack depth and normalized strength.
C-6y FE inputs and outputs across 5-500 mN were checked. The 39-289 MPa fitted-strength and 0.62-11.6 micrometer crack-depth ranges were verified.

Direct SEM evidence of the load-dependent transition from groove tension cracking to distributed ripped material; crop was rendered directly from the source PDF and visually verified.

Cross-study cement-phase comparison is not direct red-brick evidence and is summarized only as method validation.

Combined with Fig. 20 in one same-page crop to preserve low-load tension-crack, compression-damage and full-damage sequence.
Combined with Fig. 19 to show the additional high-load lateral/diagonal damage branch; crop was rendered directly from the source PDF and visually verified.
Pressure-dependent fitted strength and normalization trends are summarized as non-transferable outputs.

omitted_sections: General concrete and nanoindentation history in the Introduction

CRediT statement, declaration, data-availability statement and acknowledgments

uncertain_items.detail: The PDF clearly prints Gf = Kc^2/E/(1-nu^2). This is visually legible but differs in operation order from the common effective-modulus expression. Rounded table values do not uniquely resolve the intended calculation.

The scratch-toughness estimator assumes horizontal crack propagation, whereas the three-dimensional model and SEM interpretation show downward/slanted and later lateral cracks. Calibration absorbs part of this discrepancy, but transfer to red brick is not established.

The authors report phase-resolved Kc as nearly force-independent to 100 mN despite acknowledging increasing phase interaction; the apparent robustness is specific to careful posterior SEM selection and should not be generalized.
ft is fitted to peak horizontal force and varies with contact pressure/compaction. The 39-289 MPa range is a conditional model output, not an independently measured tensile material property.

No AE hits were recorded for several phases despite SEM-visible cracks. AE detectability depends on phase brittleness, scattering, damping and threshold, so absence of hits cannot be treated as absence of damage.

known_warnings: Cement-paste phase properties and load thresholds are not direct parameters for fired-clay brick.
The finite-element model assumes a homogeneous phase, infinitely rigid indenter, full rigid contact in the scratch direction and prescribed displacement.
The numerical model stops at first-crack initiation and early propagation and does not simulate complete debris detachment or re-contact.
Damage-zone width depends on the maximum element size even though crack-band regularization is used for energy objectivity.
The extracted figures were visually checked at high resolution; labels, units, scales and captions are legible and no key semantic element is clipped.
All 22 pages were visually reviewed through rendered page sheets; retained equations and critical tables were additionally checked on 300-dpi page renders.
character_count_method: Count of CJK Unified Ideographs after excluding display-formula blocks, Markdown table rows and image-link rows; within the allowed 3500-character extension because the paper contains independent scratch-calibration and three-dimensional damage submodels.
